title: A Cool Music Show with Stormzy's Vest
summary: There is a new music show in London called The Music Is Black: A British Story. It is at the V&A East museum in Olympic Park. The show has many special items from black British musicians over 125 years. One big item is Stormzy's stab-proof vest. He wore it at Glastonbury, a big music festival. The vest was made by Banksy and has the Union Jack flag on it. It shows how music talks about important things like safety and being proud of Britain. Other items include Joan Armatrading's guitar, clothes from Seal and Poly Styrene, and Michael Jackson's handwritten Thriller lyrics. The show tells how music from Africa and the Caribbean came to the UK and changed into new styles like jazz, reggae, and grime. It helps people learn about history and how music can share stories and fight problems.

background_read: This show is about black British music, which means music made by black people in Britain. It started over 100 years ago with styles like jazz and reggae from Africa and the Caribbean. Over time, it mixed with British culture to create new sounds like grime. Musicians like Stormzy use their music to talk about important topics like safety and identity. The show helps kids learn how music can tell stories and bring people together, showing that art can help us understand different lives and histories.
Article_Structure: WHO: Black British musicians like Stormzy, Joan Armatrading, Seal, Poly Styrene, and Michael Jackson.
WHAT: A new exhibition called The Music Is Black: A British Story showing items from 125 years of black British music.
WHERE: At the V&A East museum in London's Olympic Park.
WHY: To celebrate and teach about black British music history and how it addresses issues like identity and prejudice.

perspectives.description: The show highlights the achievements and contributions of black British musicians over time.
Items like Stormzy's vest show how music can speak out against problems like crime and discrimination.
